title: Anthropic Mythos Expansion Opens a New AI Cybersecurity Market
author: Opeyemi Babalola
description: Anthropic expanded Project Glasswing to 150+ organizations across 15+ countries, positioning its Mythos AI capabilities as an enterprise cybersecurity solution ahead of its planned October 2026 IPO. The expansion demonstrates proof of enterprise adoption with 10,000+ vulnerabilities identified, while creating competitive pressure on traditional vulnerability scanning vendors and establishing Anthropic's foothold in critical infrastructure security globally.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Primary cloud and training partner for Anthropic, positioned to benefit from increased AI infrastructure demand as Anthropic scales enterprise security offerings
Holds five-gigawatt TPU compute agreement with Anthropic, benefits from infrastructure partnership as Anthropic expands globally
Holds five-gigawatt TPU compute agreement with Anthropic, benefits from infrastructure partnership as Anthropic expands globally
Joined Anthropic Series H as strategic infrastructure partner, positioned to supply memory/compute components for expanded AI security deployments

Integrated with Claude Security API but faces potential disruption from Anthropic's reasoning-based replacement approach to pattern-matching tools; may benefit from differentiation or face displacement risk

Connected to Claude Compliance API but faces similar competitive threat from Anthropic's AI-driven vulnerability scanning; cybersecurity integrators may see product differentiation benefits or displacement
Integrated with Claude Security ecosystem but exposed to disruption risk from AI-based security solutions replacing traditional pattern-matching approaches
Pure-play security vendor facing direct disruption threat from Anthropic's Mythos capabilities positioned as replacement for traditional vulnerability scanning tools

title: Dell, Snowflake, And Okta Are Among Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (May 25-May 29): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Ten large-cap stocks were top performers last week, with Dell Technologies leading at 57% gain after beating Q1 expectations and raising FY27 guidance. Snowflake jumped 52.33% following strong Q1 results and a strategic AWS partnership. Other notable gainers included Okta (36.5%), NetApp (28.8%), Best Buy (27.37%), and Ford (22.64%), all driven by better-than-expected earnings or positive guidance updates.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: 57% weekly gain driven by better-than-expected Q1 financial results, above-estimate Q2 guidance, and raised FY27 guidance
52.33% weekly gain following better-than-expected Q1 results and multi-year strategic collaboration agreement with AWS for enterprise AI

28.8% weekly gain following better-than-expected Q4 results and above-estimate Q1 and FY27 guidance

title: Consumer Tech News (May 25-29): Dell, IBM, CrowdStrike, Meta Lead Big Tech AI Push
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Major tech companies drove AI innovation this week with strong earnings and strategic initiatives. Dell reported record Q1 revenue of $43.84B, significantly beating estimates, while IBM and Red Hat launched a $5B open-source security initiative. Meta expanded into paid subscriptions across its platforms, and CrowdStrike expanded its cybersecurity initiatives. Anthropic surpassed OpenAI as the world's most valuable startup at $965B valuation. Japanese banks gained access to OpenAI's GPT-5.5 model for cybersecurity defense.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Reported Q1 revenue of $43.84B, significantly exceeding analyst estimates of $35.45B, with adjusted EPS of $4.86 vs. expected $2.94. Secured a $9.7B Pentagon contract for Microsoft software licensing management.
Launched Project Lightwell, a $5B initiative to secure open-source software against AI-driven cyber threats, demonstrating strategic investment in emerging security needs.
Expanding Project QuiltWorks initiative with major cyber insurance industry players, strengthening market position in cybersecurity solutions.
Introducing paid subscriptions across Instagram, Facebook, WhatsApp, and Meta AI to unlock new AI-driven revenue streams and deepen monetization.

title: Why Okta Stock Surged to a New 52-Week High Today
author: Joe Tenebruso
description: Okta stock surged 30.43% to a new 52-week high after reporting strong Q1 FY2027 earnings with 11% YoY revenue growth to $765 million and beating EPS estimates at $0.91 vs. $0.85 expected. The company generated $271 million in free cash flow and guided for full-year revenue growth of ~10% to $3.2 billion. CEO Todd McKinnon highlighted the company's strategic positioning to secure and govern AI agents as a new workforce, positioning Okta as a critical cybersecurity provider in the agentic AI era.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong earnings beat with 11% YoY revenue growth, impressive free cash flow generation ($271M in Q1), positive forward guidance, and strategic positioning in the high-growth agentic AI market. Stock surged 30.43% on the news, reflecting strong investor confidence in the company's growth prospects and competitive moat in AI security.

title: Okta Shares Climb After Strong Q1 Results, Forward Guidance
author: Erica Kollmann
description: Okta reported Q1 earnings of $0.91 per share, beating the consensus estimate of $0.85, with revenue of $765 million exceeding the Street estimate of $751.88 million. The company raised its fiscal 2027 guidance for adjusted EPS to $3.79-$3.87 and revenue outlook to $3.185-$3.205 billion. The stock climbed 9.59% to $103 in extended trading following the announcement.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Okta exceeded both earnings and revenue estimates, raised full-year guidance above analyst expectations, demonstrated strong RPO growth of 16% year-over-year, and the stock surged 9.59% in after-hours trading. The company is positioning itself well in the emerging AI agents market as an identity provider.
